title: SoundHound AI (SOUN) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: SoundHound AI reported strong Q1 2026 results with $44.2 million in revenue, representing 52% year-over-year growth. The company's automotive and IoT AI segment grew 88% organically, excluding acquisition impacts. Key financial metrics included GAAP gross margin of 31% and non-GAAP gross margin of 50%. Operating expenses increased across R&D ($26.2 million, up 6%), Sales & Marketing ($19.2 million, up 60%), and G&A ($25.7 million), primarily driven by acquisition-related costs. The company announced a definitive agreement to acquire LivePerson, positioning the combined entity to reach $500 million in revenue based on existing customer relationships. Management projects 2027 post-acquisition revenue between $350-$400 million, including minimum $100 million from LivePerson's customer base. SoundHound unveiled OASIS, its orchestrated agent platform enabling AI-built AI for multichannel deployment. Leadership emphasized proprietary foundation model investments capped at under 1% of market cap, designed to reduce dependency on third-party frontier models and improve margins. The company maintained a strong balance sheet with $216 million in cash and equivalents with no debt. Management highlighted major customer wins including agreements with Walmart's ONN brand and Fortune 100 companies across automotive, financial services, healthcare, and retail sectors. For 2026, the company projects revenue between $225-$260 million, with expectations for seasonality to normalize as recurring business grows.
